ground_truth.title: Why Cheap SEO Doesn't Work
ground_truth.author: John Crenshaw
ground_truth.main_content: In 2006, I accepted a job offer with a mortgage company in Southern California where guys were making $80,000 a month. The company was hiring anyone with confidence, and after about a month I realized they were scamming people. When the mortgage industry collapsed in 2007, I moved on. A year later, one of my old co-workers called me about recruiting me for SEO work. Those same guys who were scamming 70-year old ladies out of their homes were now working in SEO. SEO is a great business for these guys because companies all over the country need SEO work, but many don't have a clue how SEO works or how much it should cost. They get calls from companies selling SEO for a couple hundred bucks a month and it's too good an offer to pass up. They spend the money for 6 months, see no results, then hire another company. This is why around 80% of all non-referral clients come to us after working with another company. Half the companies I speak with that got burned could have avoided all that wasted time and money had they remembered one simple life lesson: If it's too good to be true then it probably is. SEO is quite a bit more than just getting ranked in Google for a set of keywords. It involves on-site technical audit, customer research, keyword research and mapping, content, link/citation building and PR, and reporting. Low-cost providers can't do these effectively because a thorough site audit on a small, simple website takes at least 10-15 hours, and they have to take shortcuts. Additionally, the low cost approach completely misses the more specific, longer keywords that make up 70% of all search traffic. If you're paying a company a few hundred dollars a month for SEO, at typical hourly rates ($100-150 per hour), you'll get about 2 hours of work. At that rate, it would take 5-8 months just to do the site audit on a simple site. Google is now actively penalizing websites that were taking shortcuts before. The next time you get a call from someone offering front page rankings for $200 a month, ask yourself, Does this even make sense?
with: SEO is a great business for these guys because companies all over the country have realized they need, at least to some extent, SEO work.
Half the companies I speak with that got burned could have avoided all that wasted time and money had they remembered one simple life lesson: If it's too good to be true then it probably is.
SEO is quite a bit more than just getting ranked in Google for a set of keywords.
Additionally, the low cost approach completely misses the more specific, longer keywords that make up 70% of all search traffic.
Google is now actively penalizing websites. They never did this in the past.
